skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Apply Anthropic brand colors, typography, and visual style guidance to presentations, documents, diagrams, and other artifacts.
skill.summary: Helps format artifacts with Anthropic colors, fonts, and visual hierarchy.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged inline Markdown color values, style terms, and class names as command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Review of SKILL.md found documentation only, with no executable code, shell commands, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempts. All detected patterns are false positives, and no semantic threats were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Color Codes Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated inline backticks around hex color values as Ruby or shell execution. The cited lines are Markdown list items defining brand colors, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks only delimit literal hex color values in Markdown. There is no command interpreter, script block, or user input path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Brand Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged descriptive brand and typography text as weak cryptography. The cited lines describe the skill, keywords, and font fallbacks, with no cryptographic function or hash operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain prose about brand styling and fonts. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic API usage is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Terms Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a gray color label and RGBColor documentation as reconnaissance. The cited lines only describe visual styling values and color application details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms appear in visual design context, not system or network discovery. No command, URL, socket, or scanning behavior exists.

content.value_statement: Teams often need consistent Anthropic-style visuals across documents and presentations. This skill provides practical color, typography, and accent guidance for polished branded artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Provides Anthropic-inspired dark, light, gray, orange, blue, and green color values.
Specifies Poppins for headings and Lora for body text with common font fallbacks.
Guides font choices based on heading size, body text, and text hierarchy.
Recommends accent colors for non-text shapes and visual elements.
Documents RGB color use for consistent visual matching across artifacts.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automatic artifact conversion tools.
Requires the user or host tool to apply styles to the target artifact.
Exact typography depends on Poppins and Lora being installed locally.
Focuses on visual styling, not legal approval for brand usage.

use_cases.title: Format a presentation deck
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, heading fonts, body fonts, and accent colors to a slide deck.

use_cases.title: Style a product document
use_cases.description: Align headings, body text, and callout elements with Anthropic-inspired visual standards.

use_cases.title: Prepare branded diagrams
use_cases.description: Choose approved-looking colors and typography for diagrams, flowcharts, and visual explanations.

prompt_templates.title: Style a simple document
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short written artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the brand-guidelines skill to this document. Update headings, body text, colors, and accents using the documented style rules.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a slide deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this slide deck for brand consistency. Recommend specific typography, color, and shape updates for each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Create a visual style plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before producing a new artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a style plan for a new executive briefing using the brand-guidelines skill. Include palette, typography, hierarchy, and accent usage.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a finished artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this artifact against the brand-guidelines skill. List inconsistent colors, typography issues, weak hierarchy, and practical fixes.

output_examples.input: A slide deck with mixed fonts, bright colors, and inconsistent callout shapes.
output: Use Poppins for slide titles and Lora for body text.
Replace bright backgrounds with the documented light background color.
Use orange, blue, and green accents for shapes in a controlled rotation.

output_examples.input: A technical document that needs an Anthropic-inspired visual style.
output: Set large headings in Poppins with strong contrast.
Keep paragraph text in Lora or Georgia for readability.
Use subtle gray backgrounds for secondary sections and callouts.

output_examples.input: A process diagram with many unrelated colors.
output: Limit shape colors to the documented accent palette.
Use dark text on light backgrounds for clarity.
Reserve the primary orange accent for the most important path.

best_practices: Start with the documented palette before adding any custom color.
Use heading and body fonts consistently across the full artifact.
Check contrast and readability after applying brand colors.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the guidance as permission to use protected brand assets.
Do not mix many additional accent colors into the same artifact.
Do not depend on custom fonts without checking local font availability.

faq.question: What does this skill help style?
faq.answer: It helps style presentations, documents, diagrams, and similar artifacts using Anthropic-inspired colors and typography.

faq.question: Does it install fonts?
faq.answer: No. It recommends Poppins and Lora, with Arial and Georgia as fallbacks when those fonts are unavailable.

faq.question: Does it contain automation scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and does not include executable scripts or command workflows.

faq.question: Can it apply styles automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance. The host tool or user must apply the style changes to the target artifact.

faq.question: Is this an official Anthropic brand source?
faq.answer: No. It is a community skill that summarizes visual styling guidance for Anthropic-inspired artifacts.

faq.question: What should I provide with my prompt?
faq.answer: Provide the artifact, target format, audience, and any constraints for fonts, colors, or accessibility.
